Title: Molecular insights into the regulation of flavonoid biosynthesis in fruits

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Canonical CsTT8 promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis and vacuolar transport in blood orange, whereas the alternatively spliced isoform (-15-CsTT8) negatively regulates pigmentation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Alternative splicing analysis; protein interaction/localization assays as summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTT8 modulates anthocyanin biosynthesis and transport into vacuoles in blood orange. 2. An alternatively spliced transcript lacking one exon negatively regulates pigmentation. 3. The splice isoform cannot interact with CsMYBs or localize to the nucleus and may feedback-limit CsTT8 expression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene here because the review emphasizes its transport-associated/MBW partner role rather than catalytic activity or standalone master regulator status.
Common_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback regulation of CsTT8 expression proposed for -15-CsTT8
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus for canonical form; splice isoform not nuclear

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes peel coloring by mediating DNA methylation status of anthocyanin structural genes.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcMET1 is a DNA methyltransferase associated with fig peel coloring. 2. It mediates DNA methylation state of anthocyanin structural genes. 3. This regulation is linked to anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: DNA methylation-mediated control of anthocyanin structural genes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported in review as promoting peel coloring despite being a methyltransferase; exact locus-specific methylation effect should be interpreted from cited primary study.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates apple peel coloration by promoting ubiquitination and degradation of MdMYB1; also part of UVR8-COP1-HY5-MYB light signaling module.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction and degradation assays summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCOP1 interacts with MdMYB1 and promotes its ubiquitination and degradation. 2. This suppresses anthocyanin accumulation in apple peel. 3. In UV-B signaling, COP1 is modulated by UVR8 to relieve HY5 degradation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May broadly affect light signaling beyond flavonoids.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation by degrading MdMYB1 protein.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein degradation evidence summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMIEL1 is an E3 ligase targeting MdMYB1. 2. Degradation of MdMYB1 reduces anthocyanin accumulation in apple.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis by promoting degradation of MdWRKY40, MdMYB1, and MdMYB9 under specific cues.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/ubiquitination evidence summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBT2 promotes MdWRKY40 degradation in the absence of wounding. 2. It promotes ubiquitination and degradation of MdMYB1 in response to nitrate. 3. It also negatively regulates anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis via degradation of MdMYB9.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation by phosphorylating MdMYB1.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light induces MdMPK4. 2. Active MdMPK4 phosphorylates MdMYB1. 3. This promotes anthocyanin accumulation.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation by phosphorylating and stabilizing MdHY5, preventing MdCOP1-mediated degradation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction/phosphorylation assays summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMPK6 is induced by light. 2. It phosphorylates MdHY5 and increases MdHY5 stability. 3. Stabilized MdHY5 enhances activation of anthocyanin-related genes.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Low-temperature-induced FvMAPK3 represses anthocyanin accumulation by phosphorylating FvMYB10 and CHS1.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phosphorylation and degradation evidence summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FvMAPK3 is induced by low temperature. 2. It phosphorylates FvMYB10 to reduce transcriptional activity. 3. It also phosphorylates CHS1 to enhance proteasome-mediated degradation, reducing fruit coloration.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced fruit coloration under chilling conditions.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glucose-induced anthocyanin accumulation via phosphorylation target MdbHLH3.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Signal transduction and phosphorylation evidence summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Exogenous glucose induces anthocyanin biosynthesis via MdHXK1. 2. MdHXK1 targets MdbHLH3 for phosphorylation. 3. Phosphorylation stabilizes MdbHLH3 and increases transcription of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis under moderately low temperature by sumoylating and stabilizing MdMYB1.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdSLZ1 directly sumoylates MdMYB1. 2. Sumoylation increases MdMYB1 stability. 3. This upregulates anthocyanin biosynthesis under moderately low temperature.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides entry precursor for flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background pathway gene summarized in review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies precursor for downstream flavonoid pathway.
Generates activated precursor for chalcone formation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to produce naringenin chalcone.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key entry enzyme for flavonoid backbone synthesis.
Produces common flavanone precursor for flavonoid branches.
Provides precursor for several downstream flavonoid branches.
Directs flux toward anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis.
Produces anthocyanidin precursors for anthocyanin formation.
Terminal enzyme for stable anthocyanin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant in anthocyanin accumulation in several fruits

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts leucoanthocyanidin to flavanols leading to proanthocyanidins.
Converts dihydroflavonol into flavonols such as kaempferol, myricetin, and quercetin.
Reduces anthocyanidins to flavan-3-ols in PA biosynthesis.
Generates flavonol products leading to quercetin 3-O-glucoside and quercetin 7-O-glucoside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Key structural gene for golden Camellia flavonol biosynthesis; used to engineer yellow, quercetin-enriched tomato fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering quercetin-enriched tomato with altered yellow pigmentation and enhanced antioxidant capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CnFLS1 was identified as a key structural gene in golden Camellia flavonol biosynthesis. 2. Transformation with CnFLS1 and other structural genes produced yellow tomato fruit with quercetin-enriched flesh. 3. Engineered fruit showed enhanced antioxidant capacity and health benefits.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylate diverse flavonoids at the 7-hydroxy position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promote accumulation of flavonoid glycosides and may contribute to citrus defense against Huanglongbing infection.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Novel citrus 7GlcTs glycosylate various flavonoids at the 7-hydroxy position. 2. They promote flavonoid glycoside accumulation. 3. They may contribute to defense against Huanglongbing infection.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in conclusion/future perspective as emerging structural-modification genes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses the same substrates as stilbene synthase to support flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR knockout of CHS2 in Vitis davidii cells downregulated flavonoid accumulation and shifted metabolic flow toward resveratrol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS2 and STS compete for common substrates. 2. CHS2 knockout reduced flavonoid accumulation. 3. Metabolic flux shifted toward resveratrol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to redirect phenylpropanoid flux between flavonoids and stilbenes.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces anthocyanin biosynthesis in Asian pear.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyMYB10 induces anthocyanin biosynthesis in Asian pear. 2. It functions as a positive anthocyanin regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of pear anthocyanin pigmentation

Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation via upstream repeat units in own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in apple skin and flesh.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB10 induces anthocyanin accumulation in apple. 2. High expression in red-fleshed 'Red Field' correlates with flesh coloration. 3. MdMYB10 can bind and activate its own promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in cortex of red-fleshed cultivar and during skin/flesh color formation
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter methylation inversely correlates with transcript level and anthocyanin accumulation; affected by striping/blushing and mutants
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major determinant of red pigmentation in apple skin and flesh

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; protein stability control by ubiquitination and sumoylation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and transport into vacuoles in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB1 is a positive anthocyanin regulator. 2. Its protein accumulates in light and is degraded in dark through MdCOP1s and MdMIEL1. 3. It participates in ethylene and ABA signaling modules and is stabilized by sumoylation under moderately low temperature.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light/dark; ethylene; ABA; moderately low temperature
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MdCOP1; MdMIEL1; MdWRKY40; MdSLZ1; MdbHLH3; MdbZIP44
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: LTR retrotransposon upstream regulates peel anthocyanin; promoter methylation-associated regulation of related pigmentation loci
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major activator of apple anthocyanin pigmentation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to red pigmentation in apple skin.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYBA is one of three MYB alleles controlling red pigmentation in apple. 2. It is involved in anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple skin.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Involved in anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple skin.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB3 contributes to apple skin anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates grape anthocyanin biosynthesis via control of UFGT expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VlMYBA1 is induced by light. 2. It plays a critical role in grape anthocyanin biosynthesis by controlling UFGT gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in grape

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates grape anthocyanin biosynthesis via control of UFGT expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VlMYBA2 plays a critical role in grape anthocyanin biosynthesis by controlling UFGT gene expression.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol synthesis in grapevine.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBF1 specifically activates VvFLS1 transcription. 2. Its expression is light inducible. 3. It correlates with flavonol accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specific positive regulator of PA biosynthesis in grape.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBPA1 specifically regulates PA biosynthesis in grape. 2. It activates the promoters of VvLAR1 and VvANR.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Specific activator of grape PA pathway

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specific positive regulator of PA biosynthesis in grape.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBPA2 specifically regulates PA biosynthesis in grape. 2. It activates the promoters of VvLAR1 and VvANR.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; feedback loop with repressor
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin pathway genes; CsMYB3 activation mentioned
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin accumulation in citrus; confers red blood orange flesh when induced by promoter retrotransposon.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsRuby1 activates anthocyanin accumulation in citrus. 2. A promoter LTR retrotransposon (Tcs2) increases its expression and confers blood orange red flesh. 3. Cold-responsive ERFs activate CsRuby1 via the retrotransposon. 4. CsRuby1 activates CsMYB3, forming an activator–repressor loop.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: LTR retrotransposon Tcs2 insertion in promoter elevates expression and confers cold dependency

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin accumulation in citrus.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsRuby2 can activate anthocyanin accumulation in citrus species.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of anthocyanin production in ripened strawberry fruit receptacles.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB10 regulates flavonoid metabolism in ripened strawberry fruit. 2. Its expression in receptacles is regulated by auxin and ABA. 3. Transient silencing significantly inhibits anthocyanin production. 4. Transposon insertion/truncation or promoter changes alter red/white fruit phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Gypsy transposon insertion in coding region truncates protein causing white fruit; CACTA-like transposon insertion in promoter elevates expression and confers red flesh
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major determinant of strawberry anthocyanin pigmentation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in nectarine/peach.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB10 positively regulates anthocyanin via transactivation of UFGT and DFR. 2. In blood-flesh peach, PpBL-PpNAC1 heterodimer activates PpMYB10.1. 3. Promoter deletion enhances promoter activity and correlates with red flesh phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: 487-bp deletion in promoter enhances promoter activity and correlates with red flesh
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of peach anthocyanin genes

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in mangosteen.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmMYB10 positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in mangosteen.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in Chinese bayberry.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MrMYB1 positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in Chinese bayberry.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates PA synthesis by controlling DFR and LAR in nectarine.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYBPA1 regulates PA synthesis in nectarine. 2. It controls DFR and LAR.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates PA biosynthesis in persimmon flesh.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DkMYB4 regulates PA biosynthesis in persimmon. 2. It directly acts on promoters of PA pathway genes.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of PA synthesis in persimmon.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DkMYB2 activates transcription of DkANR and DkLAR to promote PA biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavonol production in pear.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbMYB12b promotes quercetin and isorhamnetin glycoside production. 2. It activates PbCHSb and PbFLS.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates flavonol pathways in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB22 activates flavonol pathways in apple.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls flavonol and hydroxycinnamic acid biosynthesis in sweet orange.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYBF1 controls flavonol and hydroxycinnamic acid biosynthesis in sweet orange.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically regulates flavonol biosynthesis in peach fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB15 specifically regulates flavonol biosynthesis in peach fruit.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically regulates flavonol biosynthesis in peach fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYBF1 specifically regulates flavonol biosynthesis in peach fruit.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates both anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis in pear.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbMYB10b induces PbDFR expression. 2. It regulates anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PA pathway and also stimulates anthocyanin and flavonol production in pear.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbMYB9 specifically activates the PA pathway through PbANR. 2. It also stimulates anthocyanin and flavonol production via PbUFGT1.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates flavonol biosynthesis and other flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB17 promotes flavonol biosynthesis and other flavonoid accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates PA synthesis and, with MeJA, promotes anthocyanin and PA accumulation in apple calli.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB9 positively regulates PA synthesis by activating the ANR promoter. 2. MeJA-induced MdMYB9 together with MdMYB11 promotes anthocyanin and PA accumulation. 3. MdMYB9 interacts with MdbHLH3 and is subject to MdBT2-mediated degradation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin and PA accumulation in MeJA-treated apple calli.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB11 together with MdMYB9 promotes anthocyanin and PA accumulation under MeJA. 2. It binds promoters of MdANS, MdANR, and MdLAR and interacts with MdbHLH3.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induces anthocyanin and flavonol compounds.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of VvMYB5a strongly induces anthocyanin and flavonol compounds in tobacco.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin and PA production.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYB5b promotes anthocyanins and PA production in transgenic tobacco flowers.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances PA synthesis through interaction with bHLH partners.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB12 interacts with bHLH3 and bHLH33. 2. These interactions enhance PA synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms MBW complex with MYBs to regulate pear anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpbHLH3 interacts with PpMYB10 and PpMYB114. 2. These MBW complexes regulate anthocyanin biosynthesis in pear fruit.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synergistically promotes myricetin derivative accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MrbHLH2 acts with MrMYB12 and MrMYB5L to induce higher accumulation of myricetin derivatives.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulates quercetin biosynthesis and, with partners, promotes myricetin derivative accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MrMYB12 activates the MrFLS2 promoter to upregulate quercetin biosynthesis. 2. Synergistic action with MrMYB5L and MrbHLH2 induces higher myricetin derivative accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates apple anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB6 negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB16 negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB15L negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses flavonol and anthocyanin pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB1 represses quercetin/flavonol and anthocyanin pathways in transgenic tobacco.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin pathway and regulates branching point between anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcMYB1 represses the anthocyanin pathway. 2. It regulates the branching point of anthocyanin/PA biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates grape anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYB4-like negatively regulates anthocyanin or flavonoid biosynthesis in grape.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulate anthocyanin biosynthesis in grape.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBC2-L1/L2/L3 are negative regulators of anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulate flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpMYB17-20 negatively regulate flavonoid biosynthesis in peach flowers.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression upregulates anthocyanin and flavonoid accumulation in apple calli.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRKY11 overexpression enhances anthocyanin and flavonoid accumulation in apple calli.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses flavonol biosynthesis in grape berries.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvWRKY70 represses flavonol biosynthesis in grape berries. 2. Light and high temperature downregulate VvWRKY70 expression.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flavanone and flavonol biosynthesis and enhances powdery mildew resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of VqWRKY31 enhances PM resistance. 2. It increases salicylic acid and ROS levels. 3. It promotes biosynthesis of flavanones and flavonols.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-associated flavonoids contributing to powdery mildew resistance

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases PA content and powdery mildew resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of VqWRKY56 increases PA content. 2. It enhances powdery mildew resistance. 3. It interacts with VqbZIPC22 in planta.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases anthocyanin and PA levels in kiwifruit calli.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcWRKY44 increases anthocyanin and PA accumulation in kiwifruit calli.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synergistically promotes PA-mediated powdery mildew resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VqbZIPC22 interacts with VqWRKY56 in planta. 2. Together they synergistically promote PA-mediated powdery mildew resistance.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockout promotes anthocyanin accumulation and inhibits flavonol synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR knockout of VvbZIP36 promotes anthocyanin accumulation in leaves. 2. It inhibits flavonol synthesis. 3. The gene therefore acts as a negative regulator of anthocyanin and positive regulator of flavonol branch.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and carbon flux redirection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates primary and secondary metabolism, directing carbon flux toward flavonoid and phenylalanine metabolites.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpbZIP44 positively regulates primary and secondary metabolism. 2. It directs carbon flux toward accumulation of flavonoid and phenylalanine metabolites.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances flavanone and flavone accumulation in citrus by activating CHI expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitERF32 is one of three AP2/ERF TFs that activate CHI expression. 2. This enhances flavanone and flavone accumulation in citrus.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances flavanone and flavone accumulation in citrus by activating CHI expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitERF33 activates CHI expression to enhance flavanone and flavone accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances flavanone and flavone accumulation in citrus by activating CHI expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitRAV1 activates CHI expression to enhance flavanone and flavone accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and carbon flux direction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs carbon flux for flavonoid synthesis in navel orange.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsERF003 specifically directs carbon flux for flavonoid synthesis in navel orange.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stimulates flavonol accumulation in apple flesh in response to visible light.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdAP2-34 responds to visible light. 2. It activates MdF3H and stimulates flavonol accumulation in apple flesh.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdERF109 directly binds promoters of MdCHS, MdUFGT, and MdbHLH3. 2. It promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces anthocyanin biosynthesis in fig.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcMADS9 induces anthocyanin biosynthesis in fig. 2. Ethylene promotes this effect.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for ALA-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple skin.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 5-aminolevulinic acid-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis requires MdMADS1 function and activity.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts upstream of CitRuby1 to promote anthocyanin accumulation in juvenile citrus tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CitWRKY75 acts upstream of CitRuby1. 2. It promotes transient anthocyanin accumulation in various juvenile tissues.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Regulates MYBs during ripening and impacts anthocyanin accumulation in bilberry.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VmTDR4 regulates MYBs in ripening bilberry. 2. This impacts anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PpMYB10.1 with PpNAC1 to stimulate blood-flesh peach anthocyanin pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpBL forms a heterodimer with PpNAC1. 2. The heterodimer activates PpMYB10.1. 3. This stimulates anthocyanin pigmentation and is repressed by PpSPL1.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates PpMYB10.1 with PpBL to stimulate blood-flesh peach anthocyanin pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpNAC1 forms a heterodimer with PpBL to activate PpMYB10.1 and anthocyanin pigmentation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses the transactivation activity of the PpBL-PpNAC1 heterodimer, limiting anthocyanin pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpSPL1 represses the PpBL-PpNAC1 heterodimer activity.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanin inhibitor in apple flesh.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHB1 interacts with MBW components and constrains the complex in the cytoplasm. 2. This represses MdDFR and MdUFGT transcription and inhibits anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedback regulation of its own expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induces anthocyanin and PA synthesis in grape berry skin and seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYC1 interacts with various VvMYBs. 2. It induces anthocyanin and PA synthesis in berry skin and seeds. 3. It shows feedback regulation of its expression.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis by activating MdMYB10 and interacting with MdMYB10-containing MBW complex.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdAP2_1a positively regulates MdMYB10 transcription. 2. It also interacts with MdMYB10 to promote anthocyanin biosynthesis. 3. It is the target of miR172.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key node linking miRNA control to apple anthocyanin regulation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in kiwifruit flesh by stabilizing MBW complex formation and activating AcbHLH1.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcMADS68 interacts with AcMYBF110 and AcMYB123 to stabilize MBW complex formation. 2. It directly activates the AcbHLH1 promoter. 3. These actions amplify anthocyanin biosynthesis in kiwifruit flesh.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis and transport in red-skinned pear.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyWRKY26 forms a complex with PybHLH3. 2. The complex co-targets the PyMYB114 promoter. 3. This promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis and transport.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin biosynthesis to maintain homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsMYB3 is transcriptionally activated by CsRuby1. 2. It represses the activity of the CsRuby1/CsbHLH1 complex. 3. Together with CsRuby1 it forms a loop maintaining anthocyanin homeostasis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Homeostatic repressor preventing excess citrus anthocyanin

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes light-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis in grape berries.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Light activates the VvHY5-VvMYBA1 module. 2. VvHY5 binds the VvMYB24 promoter to activate transcription. 3. This promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback after anthocyanin threshold is reached
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses grape anthocyanin biosynthesis in a light-triggered negative feedback loop.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvMYBA1 activates VvBBX44 expression after anthocyanin reaches a threshold. 2. VvBBX44 represses VvHY5 and VvMYBA1 transcription. 3. This forms a negative feedback loop controlling anthocyanin homeostasis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses excess anthocyanin production in red-skinned pear fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PyMYB107 is activated by the PyMYB10/MYB114-PybHLH3 complex. 2. It competitively binds PybHLH3 and interferes with MBW complex stability. 3. This represses excess anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback within MBW regulatory circuit

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin by targeting repressor VvMYB114.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR828 targets VvMYB114 repressor. 2. This promotes anthocyanin accumulation in grape.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin by targeting repressor VvMYB114.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR858 targets VvMYB114 repressor. 2. This promotes anthocyanin accumulation in grape.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces red coloration and anthocyanin content.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of miR172 reduced red coloration and anthocyanin content in apple. 2. Its target is MdAP2_1a.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis by cleaving MdPIF1 mRNA.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. vsiR693 is a viroid-derived siRNA identified in apple. 2. It targets MdPIF1 mRNA for cleavage. 3. This negatively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FLS; CHS; repression of MdWRKY41 in module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Central positive regulator in UV-B-, light-, and hormone-responsive flavonoid modules.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In the UVR8-COP1-HY5-MYB cascade, MdHY5 activates FLS and CHS together with MYB10 or MYB22. 2. MdMPK6 phosphorylation stabilizes MdHY5. 3. MdHY5 represses MdWRKY41 to promote anthocyanin and PA accumulation. 4. It acts downstream of MdAGL9 in strigolactone signaling.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin and PA accumulation; suppression by MdHY5 relieves this inhibition.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRKY41 interacts with MdMYB16. 2. The MdWRKY41-MdMYB16 complex represses MdMYB12, MdANR, and MdUFGT. 3. MdHY5-mediated repression of MdWRKY41 promotes anthocyanin and PA accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis and transport in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB114 positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis and transport. 2. It is transactivated by MdbZIP4-like.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis by activating MdMYB114.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdbZIP4-like transactivates MdMYB114. 2. This promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis and transport.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation and protein complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin structural gene expression in grapevine under light.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvHY5 activates VvMYB24 transcription. 2. VvMYB24 interacts with VvMYBA1. 3. The complex upregulates anthocyanin structural genes.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high-light-induced anthocyanin accumulation in pear seedlings.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ROS under high light promotes PuHB40 expression and phosphorylation. 2. Activated PuHB40 enhances PuMYB123-like transcription. 3. This increases anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates high-light-induced anthocyanin accumulation downstream of PuHB40.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PuMYB123-like is activated by PuHB40 under ROS/high light. 2. It acts with PubHLH3 in the anthocyanin regulatory cascade.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction inhibition; relieved by degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Attenuates MBW complex formation in absence of jasmonate, thereby repressing anthocyanin and PA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdJAZ interacts with MdbHLH3 in the absence of JA. 2. This attenuates MBW complex formation. 3. JA-triggered MdJAZ degradation releases MdbHLH3 to activate anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone-mediated anthocyanin accumulation by degrading MdSMXL8.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In presence of strigolactones, MdPRT1 ubiquitinates and degrades MdSMXL8. 2. This releases MdAGL9 from an inhibitory complex and promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits anthocyanin biosynthesis by forming inhibitory complex with MdAGL9.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdSMXL8 forms an inhibitory complex with MdAGL9. 2. Degradation of MdSMXL8 under SL signaling promotes anthocyanin accumulation. 3. MdRGL2a interferes with MdSMXL8-MdAGL9 interaction, linking GA-SL crosstalk.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation after release from inhibitory complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SL-mediated anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdAGL9 is inhibited by MdSMXL8. 2. Release from this complex enables MdHY5-mediated anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction activation; degraded upon GA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation in absence of GA; its degradation under GA represses anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdRGL2a interacts with MdWRKY75 to enhance MdMYB1 transcription. 2. It also interacts with MdMYB308 to release MdbHLH3/33. 3. GA-triggered MdSINA1 degradation of MdRGL2a suppresses anthocyanin biosynthesis. 4. It mediates GA-SL crosstalk via MdSMXL8.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates MdMYB1 transcription to promote anthocyanin accumulation in the absence of GA.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRKY75 activates MdMYB1 transcription. 2. MdRGL2a enhances this activity in absence of GA.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanin repressor released upon GA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB308 is an anthocyanin repressor. 2. MdRGL2a interaction releases MdbHLH3/33 from MdMYB308 inhibition. 3. GA signaling enables repression by promoting MdRGL2a degradation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates GA-induced repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis by degrading MdRGL2a.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Upon GA signaling, MdSINA1 ubiquitinates and degrades MdRGL2a. 2. This releases repression machinery that inhibits anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Complex formation with ARF; degraded by auxin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its degradation under auxin releases MdARF13 to repress anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdIAA121 forms a complex with MdARF13. 2. Exogenous auxin promotes MdIAA121 degradation. 3. Released MdARF13 represses anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of apple anthocyanin biosynthesis under auxin signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdARF13 directly binds the MdDFR promoter. 2. It inhibits MBW complex by interacting with MdMYB10. 3. Auxin signaling releases MdARF13 from MdIAA121 complex to repress anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis in a positive ethylene feedback loop.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdEIL1 directly transactivates MdMYB1. 2. This promotes anthocyanin accumulation and contributes to positive feedback through MdERF3-enhanced ethylene production.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports positive ethylene feedback coupled to anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB1 transactivates MdERF3. 2. MdERF3 enhances ethylene production, reinforcing anthocyanin biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates ABA-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis through MdABI5 and MdbZIP44. 2. MdABI5 transactivates MdbHLH3.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes ABA-induced anthocyanin accumulation in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA upregulates MdbZIP44 expression. 2. Enhanced MdbZIP44-MdMYB1 interactions contribute to anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Cold-responsive transcriptional activation via promoter retrotransposon
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates CsRuby1 under cold conditions, promoting anthocyanin accumulation in citrus.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsERF054 is a cold-responsive ERF. 2. It activates CsRuby1 via the retrotransposon in the promoter. 3. This promotes citrus anthocyanin accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Cold-responsive transcriptional activation via promoter retrotransposon
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates CsRuby1 under cold conditions, promoting anthocyanin accumulation in citrus.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsERF061 is a cold-responsive ERF. 2. It activates CsRuby1 via the retrotransposon in the promoter.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases anthocyanin, phenolics, and flavonoids and alters fruit physiology.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB75-OE fruits accumulate more anthocyanin, phenolics, and flavonoids. 2. Fruits show increased ethylene production and delayed ripening.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promotes anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthesis and improves yield, quality, and disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMX1 overexpression promotes anthocyanin and other flavonoid biosynthesis. 2. It also improves fruit yield, quality, and Botrytis cinerea resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target coupling nutrition and agronomic traits.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases anthocyanin in pineapple peel; promoter polymorphisms determine tissue-specific red coloration patterns.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcMYB266 overexpression increases pineapple peel anthocyanin. 2. Promoter SNPs and indels determine activation ability and coloration patterns. 3. AcMYB266 is in a small tandem MYB cluster with tissue-specific regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter SNPs and indels affect promoter activity and red coloration patterns
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for pineapple peel color engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator of anthocyanin in black pearl tomato fruits.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB7 represses SlANS expression. 2. It also interacts with bHLH proteins. 3. It is a potential tomato breeding target for anthocyanin improvement.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good candidate for editing to elevate tomato anthocyanin.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates anthocyanin under phosphate deficiency.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Phosphate deficiency-induced SlPHL1 positively regulates anthocyanin.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates flavonol accumulation in tomato pericarps.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB72 negatively regulates flavonol accumulation in tomato pericarps.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate locus promoting anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcTTG1 is a candidate locus that promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis in tobacco leaves.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis and participates in MBW stabilization.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcMYBF110 promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis in tobacco leaves. 2. It interacts with AcMADS68 in kiwifruit regulatory network.
